message:
ui: ship sample bundle + 'Try sample' button — success path lands

Iteration 9. Closes the loop ADR-006 §10's WASM-local promise still had
open: an end-to-end IPFS_BUNDLE_VERIFIED audit row, with no daemon
required.

ui/public/sample-bundle.json (new — 290 B static asset)
  - A real ruLake bundle JSON. Witness computed by a one-shot
    standalone Rust binary (sha3 0.10, identical to the host crate's
    src/bundle.rs::compute_witness). Fields:
      data_ref:     rulake://demo/sample-bundle
      dim:          128
      rotation_seed: 42
      rerank_factor: 20
      generation:   {Num: 1}
      rvf_witness:  cbbf5e6852c3e944a0ce5def90f88f09c0f44f02361655f4e0083ee524e0b3db
  - Vite copies public/* into dist/ verbatim, so the file ships as
    https://<deploy>/sample-bundle.json. The GitHub Pages URL gets a
    real bundle reachable via a single fetch.

ui/src/components/screens.jsx — IpfsFetchStrip
  - New 'Try sample' button next to 'Fetch + verify'. Resolves
    `sample-bundle.json` against `document.baseURI` (so it works
    under both `/` and `/RuLake/` Vite base paths), fetches, parses,
    runs `window.RULakeWasm.verifyBundle(bundle)`, lands an audit row
    with the same code shape as a real IPFS fetch:
      IPFS_BUNDLE_VERIFIED   ok       — witness recomputed in browser matches
      IPFS_WITNESS_MISMATCH  refused  — fetch ok, hash !=
    Same trust boundary, same diagnostic discipline; only the URL
    target differs (SPA-local vs gateway-CIDpath).

E2E validated this iteration:
  $ npm run build → dist/sample-bundle.json copied 1:1 from public/
  $ agent-browser ... Bundle → 'Try sample' button
    audit#15: { tool: rulake_ipfs_fetch,
                target: http://127.0.0.1:4173/RuLake/sample-bundle.json,
                code: IPFS_BUNDLE_VERIFIED, ms: 2, outcome: ok }
    Real cryptographic verify: SHAKE-256 of the load-bearing fields
    matches the published rvf_witness byte-exactly. The 2ms is wasm-
    bound — under a single render frame.
  $ agent-browser console --errors → 0 errors

The full IPFS_* code family is now exercised across iterations 4 + 8 + 9:
  IPFS_OK                  680ms  (gateway probe — file fetched, no parse)
  IPFS_HTTP_ERROR         3032ms  (placeholder CID, 404)
  IPFS_NETWORK_ERROR         1ms  (CSP-blocked — diagnosed, fixed)
  IPFS_FETCH_FAILED        308ms  (real gateway, body not a bundle)
  IPFS_FETCH_FAILED          0ms  (sync — malformed CID)
  IPFS_BUNDLE_VERIFIED       2ms  (this iteration — success path)

Audit ledger after iteration 9 (15 rows). Every event is a real
network/crypto/dispatch operation; zero mocks anywhere in the console.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>
